items.title: USDA plans Food and Nutrition Service reorganization; SNAP leadership to relocate to Indianapolis
items.summary: USDA announced it intends to create a Food and Nutrition Administration and move nutrition program leadership and staff from Washington, D.C. to new hubs and compliance locations around the country. USDA said SNAP will be relocated to Indianapolis, with other nutrition program hubs in Dallas, Kansas City, Raleigh, and Denver, while the Administrator keeps a small presence in D.C.

items.title: CMS guidance says states must implement new limits on federal Medicaid/CHIP matching funds for certain noncitizens starting Oct. 1, 2026
items.summary: CMS issued operational guidance to prepare states for statutory changes that limit when states can claim federal matching funds in Medicaid and CHIP for individuals who are not U.S. citizens or nationals (or who fall into certain noncitizen categories). CMS said the change begins October 1, 2026, and noted exceptions including continued federal matching funds for treatment of emergency medical conditions and that the changes do not apply to the option to cover lawfully residing children and pregnant people.

items.title: SSA: Social Security and SSI recipients will get a 2.8% COLA for 2026; federal SSI maximum rises to $994/month
items.summary: The Social Security Administration announced a 2.8% cost-of-living adjustment (COLA) for 2026. SSA’s fact sheet shows the federal SSI payment standard increasing to $994 per month for an individual and $1,491 per month for an eligible couple (up from $967 and $1,450 in 2025).

items.title: USDA WIC memo sets FY 2026 cash-value benefit at $26 (children), $48 (pregnant/postpartum), and $52 (breastfeeding) per month
items.summary: USDA’s WIC Policy Memorandum #2026-2 states the FY 2026 cash-value voucher/benefit (CVV/B) inflation adjustment is 11.8% over the FY 2022 baseline. The memo lists FY 2026 monthly amounts of $26 for children, $48 for pregnant and postpartum participants, and $52 for fully or mostly breastfeeding participants.

items.title: KFF/Georgetown survey: many Medicaid expansion parents may face new work requirements starting Jan. 2027; states must add new address/death data checks
items.summary: KFF and Georgetown’s Center for Children and Families reported that many parents of older children in Medicaid expansion states may become subject to new work requirements starting January 2027, depending on whether they qualify under the mandatory Section 1931 parent pathway. The report also highlights new administrative requirements from the 2025 reconciliation law, including obtaining address updates from sources like USPS NCOA and managed care plans by January 1, 2027 and quarterly data matching with SSA’s Master Death File by January 2028.

items.title: IRS: Child Tax Credit up to $2,200 per child; refundable Additional CTC up to $1,700 (earned income must be at least $2,500)
items.summary: The IRS says the Child Tax Credit is worth up to $2,200 per qualifying child, and the refundable Additional Child Tax Credit can be up to $1,700 per qualifying child. The IRS also notes that if you claim the Earned Income Tax Credit or ACTC, refunds generally can’t be issued before mid-February.

items.title: Federal Register: Medicare Advantage/Part D final rule effective June 1, 2026 (includes insulin cost-sharing provisions for 2026+)
items.summary: The Federal Register published a CMS final rule for Medicare Advantage (Part C) and Medicare Part D with an effective date of June 1, 2026 and applicability for coverage beginning January 1, 2027. The rule text notes that for CY 2026 and subsequent years, cost-sharing for a covered insulin product is capped at the lesser of $35 or 25% of specified prices.
